steps.title: Read the original model
steps.content: From the line of best fit for data set E, two readable points are about $(0,12)$ and $(12,30)$. The intercept is about $12$.

steps.title: Find the original slope
steps.content:
Compute the slope from those two points.
$$\frac{30-12}{12-0}=\frac{18}{12}=1.5$$
So the original line is about $y=12+1.5x$.

steps.title: Scale the line by the factor
steps.content:
Multiplying every $y$-coordinate by $3.9$ multiplies both the intercept and the slope by $3.9$.
$$12(3.9)=46.8$$
The product $1.5(3.9)$ equals $5.85$, which rounds to $5.9$. The transformed line is about $y=46.8+5.9x$. The answer is A.
